_comment: PUBLIC denylist TEMPLATE for pii-guard in the open-source Edwin repo. It contains NO private entity seed -- only generic, non-sensitive tripwire tokens. Structural detection (emails, phones, API keys, /Users paths, private keys) runs WITHOUT any seed, so this file being generic does not weaken the structural gate. The authoritative private person/entity seed is injected at CI time from the PII_DENYLIST GitHub Actions secret (see README-ci.md) and never lives in this repo. Add your own private names/terms to that secret, not to this file.
_business_terms_note: Generic confidentiality markers -- if any of these literal strings appear in a public file it is almost certainly a leaked internal document and should fail the gate. Safe to publish: they are generic English phrases, not PII.
